summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting covering zoning enforcement discussions, a land use plan proposal from their planning consultant, solar panel regulation resolutions, local government fund apportionment, and personnel matters including three police officer resignations and one new hire. The board also made appointments to the Zoning Commission and BZA, accepted donations of drones and funds, and approved a fire department radio grant.
decisions: Adopted the meeting agenda
Approved payment of bills from April 11 to May 22, 2023
Adopted Resolution 2023-012 requesting Clermont County Commissioners prohibit large solar facilities and large wind farms in Goshen Township
Adopted resolution authorizing alternative method of apportionment of the undivided Local Government Fund (LGF) for calendar years 2024-2029
Approved support and resources for Memorial Day Parade and Ceremony on 5/29/23
Appointed Trustee Allen and Administrator Rose to the TIRC for 2023
Accepted $50 donation to police department from Shannon and Esther Wilder
Approved 2023-2024 SRO MOU with Goshen Local School District
Accepted resignation of Officer Tyler Kramer effective 5/21/23
Accepted resignation of Officer Matt Walker effective 5/22/23
Accepted resignation of Officer Mike Barron effective 5/26/23
Accepted donation of two drones (approx. $7,000 value) from CPAAA
Approved buyback of seven cemetery plots from Phyllis Hamblin for $875
Approved increase in revenue line item for Marshall Radio Grant of $21,580.98
Approved increase in appropriations for Marshall Radio Grant of $21,580.98
Appointed Brandon Ottey as member of Zoning Commission (term expiring 12/31/2026)
Appointed Derek Holmes as member of Zoning Commission (term expiring 12/31/2026)
Appointed Derek Holmes as member of BZA (term expiring 12/31/2027)
Appointed Gary Theodore as member of BZA (term expiring 12/31/2024)
Appointed John Langenheim as alternate on BZA (term expiring 5/23/2025)
Hired Brandon Lunsford as full-time police officer effective 5/30/2023
Approved purchase of body armor from Batavia PD for $6,000
Declined to pursue litigation against 6353 Belfast Road property owners at this time pending zoning changes review
action_items: Julia to research whether 6353 Belfast Road property meets criteria under proposed zoning changes before any litigation decision
Administrator Rose to locate Brian Shrive's draft resolution on mid-size solar panel regulation (5-49.5 megawatts) and get it to Julia/Liz for review
Liz (planning consultant) to prepare scope of work and cost estimate for land use plan update for budget discussion in fall
Chad (Service Director) to remove two remaining trees in front of building, plant grass temporarily, and plan low-growth landscaping for fall/spring
Chad to complete final document for Deerfield and Route 28 sign permit with the state
Chief Myers to coordinate drone training for firefighters and police officers
Chief Myers to continue following up with architect and construction manager on Station 18 code summary process
Fire department representatives to attend EMS award ceremony in Columbus on 5/24/23
Submit LGF resolution to auditor's office by August 1st deadline
key_amounts: $875 total for buyback of 7 cemetery plots at $125 each from Phyllis Hamblin
$50 donation to police department from Shannon and Esther Wilder
$7,000 approximate value of two drones donated by CPAAA
$21,580.98 Marshall Radio Grant for fire department radios
~$9,000 township match for radio grant (vs. $30,000 full cost)
$6,000 for body armor purchase from Batavia PD for new officer
~$8,000 increase in SRO contract with school district
$15,000-$25,000 estimated cost for basic land use plan update
$40,000-$50,000 estimated cost for comprehensive land use plan update
$67,000 referenced as cost of previous State Route 28 corridor plan
notable_topics: Zoning enforcement discussion regarding unpermitted structure at 6353 Belfast Road (Tombs property)
Land use plan update discussion with planning consultant Liz Fields
Large solar facility and wind farm prohibition resolution for Goshen Township
Mid-size solar panel (5-49.5 MW) zoning regulation discussion
Local Government Fund apportionment formula for 2024-2029
Three police officer resignations and hiring challenges in law enforcement
New police officer hire (Brandon Lunsford from Batavia PD)
Fire department EMS award recognition in Columbus
Memorial Day Parade and Ceremony planning
Zoning Commission and BZA membership reorganization
Fire department radio grant and Station 18 construction progress
Service department staffing challenges with two employees on medical leave
SRO Memorandum of Understanding renewal with Goshen Local School District
notes: Meeting started late at 7:17 PM due to a prior meeting running over. Executive session lasted from approximately 8:18 PM to 10:12 PM. There was notable tension regarding the zoning enforcement case at 6353 Belfast Road, with trustees questioning the value of pursuing litigation given recent zoning amendments were intended to address similar situations. Two paramedics (Nate Younger and Chris Sullivan) completed certification. Officers cited health care, pay, and vacation as reasons for leaving. Law enforcement recognition included Sergeant Cody Collier, Officer Laura Item, Captain Don Hampton, Detective Steve Maynard, Detective Greg McAllister, Detective Chris McMillan, and Officer Justin Porterfield for Lakeshore shootings investigation, and Officer Tyler Smith for excellence in service. Referenced tornado damage that previously affected the township.
title: BOT 5-23-23
